description: Letter from Yehuda, unknown location, to Shalom, in Jerusalem. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: No earlier than 13th century, based on handwriting, overall appearance, and linguistic evidence. The sender asks the addressee not to allow Ṣadaqa to sell anything from the estate of the deceased 'mother' (i.e., the sender's daughter), which rightfully belonged to her two orphans, Avraham and Rebecca, and to whom the sender had conveyed all of his own rights in the inheritance. (Information from Mediterranean Society, III, pp. 310, 495, and from Goitein's index cards)
description_he: מכתב מיהודה לשלום, בירושלים. ביהודית-ערבית. לא לפני המאה ה-13. השולח מבקש שלא יאפשר לצדקה למכור שום דבר מהעיזבון, השייך לשני יתומים אברהם ורבקה.
